subject: Q2 Portfolio Rebalance
body:
Hey Robert,

Just finished the Q2 rebalance analysis. We're overweight in tech by about 3.2% relative to benchmark. Recommending we trim NVDA and rotate into healthcare names.

Can you take a look at the proposed allocations before EOD?

Cheers,
Elizabeth

subject: Q2 Portfolio Rebalance
body:
Hey Liz,

Just finished the Q1 rebalance analysis. We're overweight in tech by about 3.2% relative to benchmark. Recommending we trim NVDA and rotate into healthcare names.

Can you take a look at the proposed allocations before EOD?

Cheers,
Christopher

subject: FYI: Macro outlook changed
body:
Heads up team,

The Fed minutes just dropped and it's more hawkish than expected. Chris, this directly impacts your duration positioning — might want to revisit the 10Y allocation.

I've attached the summary. Let's regroup at 3pm.

Best,
Robert

subject: FYI: Macro outlook changed
body:

subject: Quick favor
body:
Hey Tomoko, could you pull the historical drawdown data for our EM sovereign book? Need it for the board deck by Thursday.

Appreciate it!
Priya

subject: Urgent: Position sizing
body:
We need to cut the Japan exposure by half before Tokyo open. Lizzie, you have the authority on this book — can you action this ASAP? I'll explain on the call in 10 min.

William

subject: Compliance training reminder
body:
Dear Elizabeth,

This is a reminder that the annual compliance training module is due by end of month. Please complete it at your earliest convenience.

Thank you,
Alexander

subject: Lunch?
body:
James! Free for lunch today? The new place on Threadneedle St is supposed to be great. We can talk about the emerging markets thesis over food.

Let me know,
Priya

subject: Great call today
body:
Nice work on the Meridian pitch today, James. The client was clearly impressed with the factor analysis. I think we're in a strong position for the mandate.

Let's debrief tomorrow morning.

Best,
Elizabeth

body:

Let me know,
Priya

subject: Weekend reading
body:
Priya — thought you'd find this interesting. Bridgewater just published their updated macro framework. Particularly relevant given your view on the yield curve inversion.

Link attached.

Enjoy,
Robert
